name: Quad VCA
description: Four-channel voltage controlled amplifier with cascaded CV inputs and cascaded mix outputs. Each channel has variable linear-to-exponential response, a level bias knob, and a +6dB boost switch.

controls.name: CV Attenuator
controls.description: Controls amount of CV modulation applied to the channel's level. Cascades from previous channel if input unpatched.
controls.detail: 0 = no CV · full CW = unity CV

controls.name: Linear / Exponential Response
controls.description: Sets VCA response curve. Full CCW = exponential. Full CW = linear. In-between = variable blend.
controls.detail: CCW: exponential (natural loudness) · CW: linear (CV processing)

controls.name: Level 1
controls.description: Manual bias level for channel 1. Full CW ≈ +5V CV equivalent, unity gain in linear mode.

controls.name: Level 2
controls.description: Manual bias level for channel 2. Sets base gain without CV.

controls.name: Level 3
controls.description: Manual bias level for channel 3. Sets base gain without CV.

controls.name: Level 4
controls.description: Manual bias level for channel 4. Sets base gain without CV.

controls.name: BOOST Switch
controls.description: Increases output level by approximately +6dB (2:1). Useful for boosting low-level signals or mild overdrive.

controls.name: CV LED
controls.description: Bicolor LED shows post-attenuator CV signal level. Green = positive, red = negative voltage.

inputs.name: CV 1
inputs.description: CV input for channel 1 level control.

inputs.name: CV 2
inputs.description: CV input for channel 2. Normalled to CV 1 when unpatched.

inputs.name: CV 3
inputs.description: CV input for channel 3. Normalled to CV 2 when unpatched.

inputs.name: CV 4
inputs.description: CV input for channel 4. Normalled to CV 3 when unpatched.

inputs.name: IN 1
inputs.description: Audio or CV signal input for channel 1.

inputs.name: IN 2
inputs.description: Audio or CV signal input for channel 2.

inputs.name: IN 3
inputs.description: Audio or CV signal input for channel 3.

inputs.name: IN 4
inputs.description: Audio or CV signal input for channel 4.

outputs.name: OUT 1
outputs.description: Channel 1 output. Normalled to mix input of OUT 2 when unpatched.

outputs.name: OUT 2
outputs.description: Channel 2 output. Normalled to mix input of OUT 3 when unpatched.

outputs.name: OUT 3
outputs.description: Channel 3 output. Normalled to mix input of OUT 4 when unpatched.

outputs.name: OUT 4 / MIX
outputs.description: Channel 4 output. When OUT 1–3 are unpatched, serves as master mix output of all 4 channels.

behaviors.name: 4 Independent VCAs
behaviors.description: Each channel operates independently. Connect audio to IN, CV to CV input, take signal from OUT. LEVEL knob biases the gain. Response knob adjusts linear-to-exponential curve.

behaviors.name: Cascaded CV
behaviors.description: One CV controls multiple channels. Unpatched CV inputs inherit from the previous channel. Each channel's attenuator and response knob independently shape the cascaded CV amount.

behaviors.name: 4-Channel Mixer
behaviors.description: Leave OUT 1–3 unpatched; take mix from OUT 4/MIX. LEVEL knobs act as mix faders. CV inputs can automate individual channel levels.

patchIdeas.name: Envelope-controlled VCA with bias
patchIdeas.patch: Use channel 1 as a standard voice VCA with an envelope shaping the amplitude.
steps: Set LEVEL 1 fully counterclockwise so no signal leaks through when the envelope is at rest.
Set the Linear / Exponential Response knob for channel 1 fully clockwise (Linear) if you're doing clean CV work, or fully CCW (Exponential) for natural-sounding note dynamics.
Set BOOST 1 to the left (off) for unity max gain.
Patch VCO output → Quad VCA IN 1.
Patch envelope generator output → Quad VCA CV 1.
Turn the CV 1 Attenuator fully clockwise so the envelope reaches unity (watch the CV LED go full green at the peak).
Patch Quad VCA OUT 1 → your mixer or output.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 — VCO audio shaped by the envelope, peaking near the input level (or doubled if BOOST is on).
patchIdeas.hear: A clean note that rises and falls with the envelope. Linear response tracks the envelope shape literally; Exponential response gives a more musical, percussive feel. Nudge LEVEL 1 clockwise only if you want some signal bleed under the envelope (useful for drones with accents).
patchIdeas.diagram:
Quad VCA:
* Level 1: noon
* Linear / Exponential Response: Linear (CW)
- VCO (Out) -> Quad VCA (IN 1)
- Envelope (Out) >> Quad VCA (CV 1)
- Quad VCA (OUT 1) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Three-voice submix + independent fourth voice
patchIdeas.patch: Use channels 1–3 as a submix out of OUT 3, and channel 4 as a fully separate VCA out of OUT 4/MIX.
steps: Patch three sound sources → IN 1, IN 2, IN 3.
Leave OUT 1 and OUT 2 unpatched so channels 1 and 2 cascade into OUT 3.
Patch OUT 3 → mixer input A — this is your three-voice submix.
Use LEVEL 1, LEVEL 2, LEVEL 3 as manual faders for that submix (CV inputs optional).
Patch a fourth sound source → IN 4.
Patch OUT 4/MIX → mixer input B — because OUT 3 is patched, OUT 4/MIX now only carries channel 4.
Set LEVEL 4 to taste for the independent voice level.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 3 — summed mix of channels 1–3. OUT 4/MIX — channel 4 alone (the mix chain was broken by patching OUT 3).
patchIdeas.hear: Three voices glued together on one fader-set plus a fourth voice you can send somewhere else (effect send, stereo pan, separate level). Patching any OUT jack breaks the cascade above it, which is exactly how you isolate channel 4 here.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Quad VCA:
- Voice 1 (Out) -> Quad VCA (IN 1)
- Voice 2 (Out) -> Quad VCA (IN 2)
- Voice 3 (Out) -> Quad VCA (IN 3)
- Quad VCA (OUT 3) -> Mixer (In A) // 3-voice submix
- Voice 4 (Out) -> Quad VCA (IN 4)
- Quad VCA (OUT 4 / MIX) -> Mixer (In B) // independent voice

patchIdeas.name: Dynamic FM index
patchIdeas.patch: Use channel 1 as a variable attenuator on a modulator oscillator so an envelope can sweep FM depth per note.
steps: Patch modulator VCO (usually a sine) → Quad VCA IN 1.
Set the Linear / Exponential Response knob for channel 1 fully clockwise (Linear) — you want predictable CV scaling of the modulator level.
Set LEVEL 1 around 9 o'clock to set a small baseline FM index (quiet, almost no modulation at rest).
Patch envelope → Quad VCA CV 1.
Set the CV 1 Attenuator around noon to start — this controls how much the envelope opens the FM depth.
Patch Quad VCA OUT 1 → linear FM input of the carrier VCO.
Trigger notes: tweak LEVEL 1 for resting timbre, CV 1 Attenuator for how bright the attack sweep becomes.
Flip BOOST 1 on if you need more modulation range (aggressive FM).
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT 1 — the modulator oscillator, scaled by (LEVEL 1 + envelope). Patch into the carrier's FM input.
patchIdeas.hear: A timbre that starts dark and opens brighter on each note attack, then closes back down as the envelope decays — the classic evolving FM bell/brass behavior. Turn the CV 1 Attenuator clockwise for more dramatic sweeps, counterclockwise for subtle motion.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Quad VCA:
* Level 1: base FM index
- Modulator VCO (Out) -> Quad VCA (IN 1)
- Envelope (Out) >> Quad VCA (CV 1) // dynamic index
- Quad VCA (OUT 1) >> Carrier VCO (FM In)
